items.title: Trump tariffs: Here's what might get more expensive for Americans | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>'$1,000 per household': From avocados to cars, here’s a look at what could get more expensive for Americans as Trump’s tariffs come into effect.
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #usa #tariffs #trump #trumptariffs

items.title: LIVE: Opening session continues as Egypt hosts an emergency Arab summit on Palestinian territories
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The opening session continues as Egypt hosts an emergency Arab summit in Cairo to address the latest developments in the Palestinian territories. Egypt urged Arab leaders to adopt its reconstruction plan for Gaza, which would cost $53 billion and avoid resettling Palestinians, in contrast to U.S. President Donald Trump's 'Middle East Riviera' vision, according to a copy of the plan.

#Reuters #News #Live #PalestinianTerritories #ArabSummit 

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Trudeau on Canadians' tariff anger, boycotts and boos
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau was asked if Trump’s 25% tariffs on Canada were an ‘act of war’ — referring to what billionaire investor Warren Buffett recently described as tariffs and their effects. Trudeau responded and also explained to Americans that Canadians aren’t boycotting and booing them specifically, but their frustration is with the new U.S. administration and its policy.
 
#Trudeau #Canada #Tariffs #TradeWar #Trump #WarrenBuffett

items.title: The Trudeau era is ending. Here's who could be Canada's next PM | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Canada is gearing up to pick a new prime minister to replace Justin Trudeau who said he will step down after almost a decade at the helm. Experts say the next election, which may happen within weeks or months, might favor a candidate who can work with Donald Trump.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #trudeau #canada #politics

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Canada imposing 25% tariffs on billions of dollars of US imports | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Mincing no words, Canadian PM Justin Trudeau said the country will impose 25% retaliatory tariffs against C$155 billion worth of American goods, starting immediately with tariffs on C$30 billion worth of goods.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Drought in Somalia could push a million more into hunger | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Somalia could see a million more people pushed into acute hunger with a drought forecast to hit the crop cycle, the World Food Programme said. This is in addition to the emerging crisis due to aid cuts by the Trump administration. 

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #somalia #famine #donaldtrump #aid #africa

items.title: Scientists predict major quake in Chile's mineral-rich north | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Scientists predict a major earthquake in Chile's mineral-rich north, a region critical for the country's copper and lithium production. This follows a pattern of seismic activity occurring approximately every decade.
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #chile #earthquake #quake #world #environment

items.title: Trudeau: ‘This is a trade war’
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Canada's Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Ottawa is striking back immediately, after U.S. President Donald Trump launched a trade war against Mexico and Canada.
 
Trudeau said Canada was imposing immediate 25% tariffs on C$30 billion worth of U.S. imports and if need be would target another C$125 billion worth in 21 days' time.
 
#Trade #Tariffs #Canada #Trudeau #Trump #UnitedStates

items.title: Thales soars as 2024 earnings beat forecasts on defense spending | RUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Shares in France's Thales jumped more than 11% as defense and technology firm posted stronger-than-expected 2024 earnings despite losses in its space business. Thales shares had already soared alongside those of its peers after European leaders pledged to boost arms spending.
